info.title: meshmon Service API
info.description: Operator-facing HTTP API for the meshmon service. Agent-facing endpoints (Protobuf) are documented separately in `crates/protocol/proto/meshmon.proto`.

get.summary: `GET /api/agents` — return every agent known to the registry.
get.description:
The response is a flat JSON array sorted by `id` for determinism.
Empty when no agents have registered yet. Each row is stamped with
its cached reverse-DNS hostname (when present) and cold-miss IPs
are enqueued for background resolution against the caller's session.

get.summary: `GET /api/agents/{id}` — return a single agent by id.
get.description:
Returns 404 with a JSON error body when the id is not found in the
current registry snapshot. The 200-OK branch stamps the hostname
through the shared cache and enqueues a cold-miss resolution.

get.summary: `GET /api/alerts` — proxy active alerts from Alertmanager.
get.description:
Returns a normalized `Vec<AlertSummary>`. Upstream query parameters
(`active`, `silenced`, `inhibited`, `unprocessed`, `filter`) are
forwarded verbatim.

- **503** when `upstream.alertmanager_url` is not configured.
- **502** when the upstream is unreachable or returns a non-2xx status.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
Single PromQL-style label matcher expression, e.g.
`alertname="HighLatency"`. Multiple matchers must be combined into
one expression by the caller (Alertmanager accepts comma-separated
matchers inside a single `filter` value).

get.summary: `GET /api/alerts/{fingerprint}` — single alert by fingerprint.
get.description:
Fetches the full alert list from Alertmanager and filters client-side
by fingerprint (the v2 API has no single-alert endpoint).

- **404** when no alert matches the fingerprint.
- **502** when the upstream is unreachable or returns a non-2xx status.
- **503** when `upstream.alertmanager_url` is not configured.

post.summary: POST `/api/auth/login` — authenticate and issue a session cookie.
post.description:
- 200 + `LoginResponse` on success
- 401 on wrong credentials (JSON body: `{"error": "invalid credentials"}`)
- 500 only on infra failure (session store I/O, verify-task panic)

429.description: Too many attempts from this IP

post.description:
Idempotent: 200 whether or not the caller was logged in. The cookie is
cleared either way.

get.summary: `GET /api/campaigns` — filtered list of campaigns.
get.description:
`limit` is clamped server-side to 500 rows; list responses do not
populate `pair_counts` (see [`get_one`] for the per-state counts).

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Clamped to `1..=500` internally; default 100.

post.description:
Rejects blank titles and malformed destination IP strings up front
so a client mistake surfaces as 400 rather than leaking a Postgres
type error as 500. The `created_by` field is filled from the active
session principal; anonymous callers get 401.

get.summary: SSE stream of campaign lifecycle events.
get.description:
Requires an authenticated session (the enclosing router applies
`login_required!`). The response never ends on its own — the server
closes it on shutdown or when the client disconnects.

get.summary: `GET /api/campaigns/{id}` — single-row fetch + pair-state counts.
get.description:
Runs two queries (campaign fetch + `COUNT(*) GROUP BY
resolution_state`) and joins the results handler-side. A failure
on the counts query degrades gracefully: the campaign body is
still returned with an empty `pair_counts` list and the error is
logged — the campaign shell is more valuable to the UI than a 500.

delete.description:
Returns 204 whether or not the row existed (the underlying
`DELETE ... WHERE id = $1` is a no-op on an absent id).

patch.description:
Absent fields leave the underlying column untouched; explicit
`null` values are currently treated as "no change" (see
[`PatchCampaignRequest`]). Campaign lifecycle state is not
editable through this surface — use `/start` / `/stop` / `/edit`.

post.summary:
`POST /api/campaigns/{id}/detail` — enqueue detail-ping + detail-mtr
rows for a slice of `(source_agent, destination_ip)` pairs.
post.description:
Gated on `state IN ('completed','evaluated')`. The three scopes:

- `all`: every `kind='campaign'` pair whose baseline resolution
  `succeeded` or `reused`. Selects directly from `campaign_pairs`
  because an evaluation row is not required for this scope.
- `good_candidates`: pulls the persisted evaluation row and expands
  each qualifying `(source_agent, destination_agent, transit_ip)`
  triple into a pair of `(source_agent → transit_ip)` +
  `(destination_agent → transit_ip)` detail entries so both legs
  get a higher-resolution measurement. Requires a prior evaluation;
  400 with `no_evaluation` otherwise.
- `pair`: a single operator-chosen `(source_agent_id,
  destination_ip)` tuple from the request body.

All scopes flow through [`repo::insert_detail_pairs`], which owns
the transition back to `running` and emits `campaign_state_changed`
through the Postgres NOTIFY trigger. The campaign SSE listener
translates that NOTIFY into a broker broadcast for every
subscriber (same-instance and peers) — this handler does not
publish to the in-process broker directly, to avoid sending a
duplicate `state_changed` frame on the request's own instance.
No-op detail inserts (inserted == 0 or an already-`running` race)
don't touch `state`, so the trigger stays silent correctly.

400.description: Bad scope payload or no_evaluation precondition

post.description:
Adds/removes pairs on a finished campaign (`completed`, `stopped`,
or `evaluated`) and transitions it back to `running`. When
`force_measurement` is `Some(true)`, the sticky flag is flipped and
every non-delta pair is reset so the whole campaign re-runs.

Gated on `state IN ('completed','evaluated')`: re-running on an
already-evaluated campaign is allowed so operators can retune
`loss_threshold_ratio` / `stddev_weight` without editing the row. When
the campaign was in `completed`, a best-effort transition flips it to
`evaluated`; a concurrent transition that loses the gate still leaves
the evaluation row written and the SSE event fired.

Before the evaluator runs, the handler augments the active-probe
baseline set with VictoriaMetrics continuous-mesh samples for every
agent→agent pair the campaign's `measurements` rows didn't already
cover. VM-sourced rows carry `direct_source='vm_continuous'` on the
resulting `campaign_evaluation_pair_details`. When `upstream.vm_url`
isn't configured the handler silently falls back to active-probe
data only.

Returns:
* 422 (`no_baseline_pairs`) — no agent→agent baseline available,
  even after the VM fallback (or VM wasn't configured).
* 503 (`vm_upstream`) — VM was configured but the query failed
  (unreachable, non-2xx, malformed response).

get.summary:
`GET /api/campaigns/{id}/evaluation` — read-through on the persisted
evaluation row.
get.description:
404 (`not_evaluated`) when the campaign has never been evaluated.
The same snake_case envelope pattern as every other handler lets the
SPA's shared error layer branch on the stable code without parsing
prose.

get.summary:
`GET /api/campaigns/{id}/evaluation/candidates/{destination_ip}/pair_details`
— paginated detail breakdown of a single transit candidate's
per-baseline-pair scoring rows.
get.description:
Replaces the unbounded `EvaluationCandidateDto.pair_details` array
the wire used to ship inline. The endpoint applies server-side sort
(10-column whitelist), four optional runtime filters
(`min_improvement_ms`, `min_improvement_ratio`, `max_transit_rtt_ms`,
`max_transit_stddev_ms`) plus `qualifies_only`, and an opaque keyset
cursor for forward pagination. The cursor's tiebreak rides on the
post-T54 composite primary key
`(source_agent_id, destination_agent_id)`, which is unique within a
single `(evaluation_id, candidate_destination_ip)` tuple.

Error vocabulary:
- `not_found` (404): the campaign id does not exist.
- `no_evaluation` (404): the campaign has never been evaluated.
- `not_a_candidate` (404): the latest evaluation does not include
  `destination_ip` as a candidate.
- `invalid_filter` (400): `limit > 500`, or any filter value is
  non-finite (`NaN` / `Infinity`).
- `invalid_cursor` (400): cursor undecodable, or its sort column
  does not match the request's `sort` parameter.
- `invalid_sort` (400): `sort` is not one of the whitelisted columns
  (handled by serde at deserialization time).

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort column. See [`PairDetailSortCol`] for the closed list.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description:
Opaque keyset cursor returned by the previous page's
`next_cursor`. Absent on the first page.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Page size. Default 100; cap 500. Zero is allowed (returns an
empty `entries` page, useful for "just give me the total").

parameters.name: min_improvement_ratio
parameters.description:
Runtime filter: minimum `improvement_ms / direct_rtt_ms` ratio
(inclusive). Rows with `direct_rtt_ms <= 0` auto-pass — mirrors
the I2 evaluator's storage-filter semantics.

parameters.name: qualifies_only

post.summary:
`POST /api/campaigns/{id}/force_pair` — reset a single pair and
re-enter `running`.
post.description:
404 when the `(source_agent_id, destination_ip)` pair is unknown for
the campaign; 400 if the destination IP fails to parse.

get.summary:
`GET /api/campaigns/{id}/measurements` — joined campaign+measurements
feed for the Results browser's Raw tab. Paginated via a keyset
cursor; rows are ordered `measured_at DESC NULLS LAST, cp.id DESC`,
so settled rows lead each page and pending/dispatched pairs (no
`measured_at`) trail at the bottom of the first page. Pending rows
are not reachable via keyset pagination — see
[`fetch_campaign_measurements`] for the v1 contract.

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description:
Narrow to a single `measurements.protocol`. Pending/dispatched
pairs (no joined measurement) stay visible under this filter so
operators can monitor in-flight detail work.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Keyset cursor — base64(JSON) `{t, i}` pair from the previous page.

parameters.name: measurement_id
parameters.description:
Resolve a single (pair, measurement) row by measurement id —
used by the DrilldownDrawer for MTR lookup.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Defaults to 200, clamped to `[1, 1000]`.

get.summary: `GET /api/campaigns/{id}/pairs` — paginated pair list.
get.description:
Empty `state` filter expands to all six pair-resolution states.
`limit` is clamped to 5 000 rows handler-side; the repo clamps
further to its own upper bound.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of pair resolution states.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Clamped to `1..=5000` internally; default 500.

get.summary: `GET /api/campaigns/{id}/preview-dispatch-count` — dispatch estimate.
get.description:
Counts the campaign's actual `campaign_pairs` rows, splitting them
between ones resolvable from the 24 h reuse window and ones the
scheduler would dispatch fresh. Never writes.

post.description:
Returns 409 (`illegal_state_transition`) if the campaign is not in
`draft`. The scheduler picks up the newly-running campaign via its
`LISTEN` loop.

get.summary: `GET /api/catalogue` — filtered, size-bounded, keyset-paginated list.
get.description:
The handler converts [`ListQuery`] straight into [`repo::ListFilter`]
and returns a [`ListResponse`]. The repo layer orders by the selected
`(sort, sort_dir)` with `id DESC` as the invariant tiebreaker and
`NULLS LAST` for every column; `shapes` run a cheap bbox pre-filter
in SQL plus exact point-in-polygon in Rust over the returned page
(see [`super::repo::list`] for the `total`-is-approximate caveat).

The wire `after` string is decoded via [`super::sort::Cursor::decode`].
A decode error (malformed base64 or JSON) silently degrades to "no
cursor" — the handler serves the first page rather than returning a
400. The repo additionally discards a decoded cursor whose
`(sort, dir)` disagrees with the request's `(sort, dir)`, so a sort
change on the client naturally invalidates stale cursors.

parameters.name: country_code
parameters.description:
Zero-or-more ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes. ANY semantics.

CSV string; e.g. `?country_code=US,DE`. Repeat-key form
(`?country_code=US&country_code=DE`) is NOT supported — axum's
default `Query` extractor is `serde_urlencoded`, which does not
deserialize repeated keys into `Vec<T>`.

parameters.name: asn
parameters.description:
Zero-or-more ASN numbers. ANY semantics.

CSV string; e.g. `?asn=64500,64501`. See `country_code` for
rationale; repeat-key form is not accepted.

parameters.name: network
parameters.description:
Zero-or-more `network_operator` ILIKE patterns. ANY semantics.
Wildcards are the caller's responsibility.

CSV string; e.g. `?network=foo,bar`. See `country_code` for
rationale; repeat-key form is not accepted.

parameters.name: ip_prefix
parameters.description:
Optional IP prefix (CIDR or bare IP). Filters `c.ip <<= $prefix`
(contained-or-equal) when parseable so bare-host queries match
their own `/32` / `/128` row as well as CIDR prefixes; an
unparseable value is silently dropped.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
Optional `display_name` substring. Passed verbatim to the
handler, which wraps it with `%…%` before running an `ILIKE`
match, so callers send the literal substring they want to find
(e.g. `?name=Fastly`). `%` / `_` characters in the input are
intentionally not escaped — they behave as ILIKE wildcards.

parameters.name: bbox
parameters.description:
Optional bounding box as a CSV string; exactly four floats
`minLat,minLon,maxLat,maxLon`. Permissive parse — malformed
values silently yield no filter, matching `ip_prefix` semantics.

parameters.name: city
parameters.description:
Zero-or-more city names. ANY semantics; exact match.

CSV string; e.g. `?city=Berlin,Paris`. See `country_code` for
rationale; repeat-key form is not accepted.

parameters.name: shapes
parameters.description:
Zero-or-more polygon shapes (point-in-any OR semantics).

Accepts an inline JSON array of `[[lng, lat], ...]` rings, URL-
encoded into the query string. The server computes the union
bbox as a cheap SQL pre-filter and then runs exact point-in-
polygon over the returned page — see [`super::shapes`] (Task 2).

Malformed JSON is rejected with a 400 via
[`serde::de::Error::custom`], matching the `asn` CSV-of-ints
behaviour: filter *values* may be advisory elsewhere, but once a
value is present and structurally typed, a parse failure is a
caller bug we surface rather than silently drop.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description:
Sort column — defaults to [`SortBy::CreatedAt`]. `NULLS LAST`
applies; `id DESC` is the invariant tiebreaker.

parameters.name: after
parameters.description:
Opaque keyset cursor returned by a prior call's `next_cursor`.
Absent for the first page. See [`super::sort::Cursor`] for the
wire format and the server-side revalidation rules.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Clamped to `1..=500` internally; default 100.

post.description:
Tokens are concatenated with spaces and run through
[`parse_ip_tokens`]; accepted IPs become catalogue rows via
[`repo::insert_many_with_metadata`], and each newly-created id is
enqueued for enrichment. Existing rows come back under `existing`
so the UI can surface their current enrichment state without a
follow-up fetch. Rejected tokens land in `invalid`.

When the request carries a [`PasteMetadata`] block, each supplied
field applies to every accepted IP. Newly-created rows always
receive the values and have the field names appended to
`operator_edited_fields`. Existing rows receive a field only if it
is not already locked; paired fields
(`CountryCode`+`CountryName`, `Latitude`+`Longitude`) apply
atomically — if either half of a pair is locked, neither half is
written and the response's [`PasteSkippedSummary`] records a
composite `"Country"` / `"Location"` skip.

get.summary:
`GET /api/catalogue/facets` — cached aggregate facets for the
filter UI.
get.description:
Serves [`FacetsResponse`] from the TTL cache on
[`crate::state::AppState::facets_cache`] so the four-group-by
aggregation runs at most once per cache window per process. The
cache refreshes lazily on access; a DB error during refresh is
surfaced as 500 without polluting the cached value.

get.summary: `GET /api/catalogue/map` — adaptive map view.
get.description:
Returns raw rows when the filtered viewport count is at or below
[`repo::MAP_DETAIL_THRESHOLD`]; otherwise returns grid-aggregated
cluster buckets sized by [`repo::cell_size_for_zoom`]. The wire body
carries a `kind` discriminator so the client can branch on a single
field.

Differences from [`list`]:
- `bbox` is required — missing/malformed is a 400 via
  [`super::dto::MapQuery::bbox`]'s strict deserializer.
- `shapes`/`sort`/`sort_dir`/`after`/`city` are intentionally not
  part of the filter surface — see [`super::dto::MapQuery`].

parameters.name: country_code
parameters.description:
Zero-or-more ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes. ANY semantics. CSV form
(`?country_code=US,DE`) — see [`ListQuery::country_code`] for the
rationale; repeat-key form is not accepted.

parameters.name: asn
parameters.description: Zero-or-more ASN numbers. ANY semantics. CSV form.

parameters.name: network
parameters.description:
Zero-or-more `network_operator` ILIKE patterns. ANY semantics.
CSV form.

parameters.name: ip_prefix
parameters.description:
Optional IP prefix (CIDR or bare IP). Filters `c.ip <<= $prefix`;
unparseable values are silently dropped (mirrors [`ListQuery`]).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description:
Optional `display_name` substring. Same `%…%` wrap happens in
the handler before it hits the repo.

parameters.name: bbox
parameters.description:
Viewport bounds — `minLat,minLon,maxLat,maxLon`. **Required.**
A malformed or missing value surfaces as a 400 via serde, unlike
[`ListQuery::bbox`] which permissively drops the filter.

parameters.name: zoom
parameters.description:
Zoom level (0..=20). Controls the grid cell size for
cluster aggregation when the filtered count crosses
[`super::repo::MAP_DETAIL_THRESHOLD`]. See
[`super::repo::cell_size_for_zoom`] for the mapping.
Values above 20 surface as 400 — leaflet doesn't advertise
zooms beyond that on any of our tile backends.

post.description:
Each id in [`BulkReenrichRequest::ids`] is flipped back to
`enrichment_status = 'pending'` in a single
[`repo::mark_enrichment_start_bulk`] call and then pushed onto the
enrichment queue. Unknown ids silently no-op at both layers (the
bulk UPDATE matches zero rows and the runner tolerates missing ids
on dequeue), so callers may include speculative ids without
surfacing a per-id error path.

The prior mark-`pending` step makes the sweep a true safety net:
queue drops (backpressure or closed channel) no longer silently lose
re-enrich requests because the row is already `pending` with an
older `created_at`, so the runner's 30-second sweep will pick it up
on the next tick.

Returns 400 when `ids.len() > MAX_BULK_REENRICH_IDS` and 202 on
success (including the empty-`ids` case).

get.summary: SSE stream of catalogue lifecycle events.
get.description:
Requires an authenticated session (the enclosing router applies
`login_required!`). The response never ends on its own — the server
closes it on shutdown or when the client disconnects.

get.summary: `GET /api/catalogue/{id}` — single-row lookup.
get.description:
Returns 404 with a JSON error body when the id is absent. Keeping
the 404 body parallel to [`crate::http::user_api::get_agent`] so
SPA error handling stays uniform across catalogue + registry.

delete.description:
Returns 204 whether or not the row existed: [`repo::delete`] issues a
plain `DELETE ... WHERE id = $1` and surfaces only the affected-row
count, so the HTTP surface always answers 204. The
[`CatalogueEvent::Deleted`] event is broadcast only when a row was
actually removed (`rows_affected > 0`) — redundant deletes against a
missing id complete silently to avoid waking SSE subscribers on a
no-op.

patch.description: Partial update with revert-to-auto support. If a field is present in both the body and `revert_to_auto`, the revert wins and the write is suppressed (matches repo::patch semantics).

post.summary:
`POST /api/catalogue/{id}/reenrich` — enqueue a single row for a
fresh enrichment pass.
post.description:
Returns 202 Accepted when the id exists (the enrichment runner will
pick the row up asynchronously) and 404 when the id is unknown. The
existence check runs synchronously because a 404 is cheap to surface
and spares callers from an "accepted then silently dropped" UX.

Before enqueuing, the row is flipped back to `enrichment_status =
'pending'` via [`repo::mark_enrichment_start`]. This makes the sweep
a true safety net: if the bounded queue is full (or its receiver is
gone) and the enqueue drops, the row is already `pending` with an
older `created_at`, so the runner's 30-second sweep will pick it up
on the next tick instead of leaving the re-enrich request silently
stranded (sweep only scans rows in `pending`, so a still-`enriched`
row would otherwise never be retried).

get.summary:
`GET /api/history/destinations` — every destination reachable from
`source`, optionally narrowed by partial match on IP or display
name (case-insensitive).

`GET /api/history/measurements` — measurement rows (+ optional MTR
hops) for a (source, destination) range. Hard-capped at 5 000 rows
so a pathologically long history can't blow a browser tab; the
frontend surfaces the cap explicitly.

parameters.name: destination
parameters.description: Destination IP (v4 or v6 host string).

400.description: Malformed destination or invalid protocol token

get.summary: `GET /api/history/sources` — agents with at least one measurement.
SSE stream of `hostname_resolved` events scoped to this session.

200.description: SSE stream of hostname resolutions for this session

post.summary: Force a re-resolution of a single IP, bypassing cache freshness.

parameters.name: ip
parameters.description: IPv4 or IPv6 literal to re-resolve

202.description: Enqueued; event will arrive on the session's stream

get.summary: `GET /api/metrics/query` -- proxy an instant query to VictoriaMetrics.
get.description:
The query expression must reference at least one `meshmon_<ident>`
metric. Aggregators and function wrappers around meshmon metrics are
allowed. Queries that touch no meshmon metric are rejected with 400.

- **400** when the query references no `meshmon_` metric.
- **502** when the upstream is unreachable or returns an error.
- **503** when `upstream.vm_url` is not configured.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: PromQL expression (must reference a meshmon_ metric)

get.summary: `GET /api/metrics/query_range` -- proxy a range query to VictoriaMetrics.
get.description:
The query expression must reference at least one `meshmon_<ident>`
metric. Aggregators and function wrappers around meshmon metrics are
allowed. Queries that touch no meshmon metric are rejected with 400.

- **400** when the query references no `meshmon_` metric.
- **502** when the upstream is unreachable or returns an error.
- **503** when `upstream.vm_url` is not configured.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: PromQL expression (must reference a meshmon_ metric)

get.summary: `GET /api/paths/{src}/{tgt}/overview` — aggregate path-detail payload.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the time window (inclusive). Defaults to `to - 24h`.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the time window (inclusive). Defaults to now.

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description:
Optional protocol override (`icmp`, `udp`, or `tcp`). Returns 400 on
any other value.

404.description: Source or target agent not found

get.summary:
`GET /api/paths/{src}/{tgt}/routes` — return a paginated, time-filtered
list of route snapshot summaries (without hop detail).

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the time window (inclusive). Defaults to 24 h ago.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the time window (inclusive). Defaults to now.

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description: Protocol filter: icmp, tcp, or udp.

get.summary:
`GET /api/paths/{src}/{tgt}/routes/latest` — return the most recent
route snapshot for the given source, target, and protocol.

parameters.name: protocol
parameters.description: Protocol filter: icmp, tcp, or udp.

get.summary:
`GET /api/paths/{src}/{tgt}/routes/{snapshot_id}` — return a single
route snapshot by database id.

`GET /api/routes/recent` — returns the latest route snapshot per
`(source_id, target_id)` pair, newest first, up to `limit` rows
(default 10, max 100).

200.description: Recent route snapshots across all pairs

get.summary: `GET /api/session` — return the session probe body.
get.description:
Wired behind the `login_required!` layer in [`crate::http::router`],
so unauthenticated requests are short-circuited with a bare 401
before reaching this handler. The 401 response is documented here
so the generated OpenAPI schema tells SPA clients exactly what to
expect.

AgentLivenessConfig.description:
Agent-liveness thresholds copied from `[agents]` in the service
config. The SPA uses these to compute online / stale / offline state
from `AgentSummary.last_seen_at` against the wall clock at render
time, instead of reading a server-computed boolean baked in at
snapshot-refresh moment (which can lag by `refresh_interval_seconds`
and produce a brief "offline" flicker after a fresh push).

refresh_interval_seconds.description:
`[agents].refresh_interval_seconds`. The UI uses `2 *` this as
the "stale" threshold (the window during which the snapshot
could reasonably still be in-flight from the registry refresh).

target_active_window_minutes.description:
`[agents].target_active_window_minutes`. After this many minutes
without a `last_seen_at` update an agent is "offline".

AgentSummary.description:
Summary of a single agent, returned by the list and detail endpoints.

Write-only on the server (constructed and serialized, never parsed) so
only `Serialize` is derived. Catalogue-joined fields (`city`,
`country_code`, `country_name`, `asn`, `network_operator`) and the
session-scoped `hostname` are optional and skip-none so a missing
field is omitted from the JSON rather than serialized as `null`.

oneOf.description:
Geo coordinates joined from the IP catalogue. Absent when the
agent's IP is not in the catalogue or neither coord is populated.

country_code.description: ISO-3166 alpha-2 country code joined from `ip_catalogue`.

hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname joined at response time from the
`ip_hostname_cache`. Present only on a positive cache hit; absent
on negative hits and cold misses (the handler enqueues cold IPs
for background resolution through the session-scoped
[`crate::hostname::Resolver`]).

id.description: Unique agent identifier (matches the agent's `AGENT_ID` env var).

ip.description: Agent source IP (host address only, CIDR prefix stripped).

network_operator.description: Network operator / ISP name joined from `ip_catalogue`.

AlertSummary.description:
Normalized alert summary exposed by the meshmon API.

Derived from the Alertmanager v2 alert model, keeping only the fields
the frontend needs. `generatorURL` and other upstream-only fields are
intentionally dropped.

ends_at.description:
RFC 3339 timestamp when the alert resolved (may be empty/zero for
active alerts).

fingerprint.description: Alertmanager fingerprint (hex string), unique per alert group.

labels.description: Label set attached to the alert (alertname, severity, etc.).

source_hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname for the source agent IP, when cached.
Absent on cold miss and negative-cached IPs (skip-none).

starts_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when the alert started firing.

summary.description: Short human-readable summary from the `summary` annotation.

target_hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname for the target agent IP, when cached.
Absent on cold miss and negative-cached IPs (skip-none).
Also absent for `AgentOffline` alerts (no `target` label).

count.description: Number of rows with this ASN.

BulkReenrichRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /api/catalogue/reenrich`.

Best-effort bulk enqueue: each id is pushed onto the enrichment
queue without a prior existence check. Unknown ids resolve to a
no-op inside the runner (the row lookup simply returns none), so
callers may include speculative ids without surfacing a per-id
error path.

ids.description:
Catalogue row ids the operator wants to re-run through the
enrichment pipeline.

CampaignDto.description:
Wire shape for a single campaign.

`pair_counts` is populated only by the single-row GET endpoint; list
responses leave it empty to avoid an N+1 COUNT fan-out.

completed_at.description: When all pairs reached a terminal state, if ever.

created_by.description: Session principal that created the row; audit-only.

evaluated_at.description: When the evaluation pass last produced results, if ever.

force_measurement.description:
When `true`, the scheduler forces a fresh measurement instead of
reusing a matching row from the 24 h window.

loss_threshold_ratio.description: Loss-rate threshold (fraction 0.0–1.0) used by the evaluator.

max_transit_rtt_ms.description:
Optional eligibility cap on composed transit RTT (ms). When
set, the evaluator drops `(A, X, B)` triples whose
`transit_rtt_ms` exceeds the cap before counter accumulation.

max_transit_stddev_ms.description: Optional eligibility cap on composed transit RTT stddev (ms).

min_improvement_ms.description:
Optional storage floor on absolute improvement (ms). Combined
with [`Self::min_improvement_ratio`] under OR semantics; the
evaluator persists a `pair_details` row only when at least one
set knob's threshold is cleared.

min_improvement_ratio.description:
Optional storage floor on relative improvement (fraction
0.0–1.0). See [`Self::min_improvement_ms`] for OR semantics.

prefixItems.description:
Terminal or in-progress state of a single `(source_agent_id,
destination_ip)` pair within a campaign.

pair_counts.description: Per-state pair counts. Empty on list responses; populated on single-row GET.

protocol.description: Probe protocol shared by every pair.

started_at.description: When the campaign most recently transitioned to `running`.

stddev_weight.description: Weight applied to RTT stddev by the evaluator.

stopped_at.description: When the operator last stopped the campaign, if ever.

CampaignMeasurementDto.description:
One row for the Raw tab OR for the DrilldownDrawer's MTR resolution.

Every field but `pair_id`, `source_agent_id`, `destination_ip`,
`resolution_state`, and `pair_kind` is nullable — a `campaign_pairs`
row with `pending` or `dispatched` state has no joined `measurements`
row yet, but the Raw tab still renders it so operators can monitor
in-flight detail work.

`mtr_hops` is inlined rather than referenced by id so the
DrilldownDrawer can render MTR directly from this endpoint — there
is no separate `GET /api/measurements/:id` in the service. The
`Option<sqlx::types::Json<_>>` wrapper is mandatory for decoding
JSONB; serde renders it as a bare JSON array on the wire.

destination_hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname for the destination IP, when cached.
Absent on cold miss and negative-cached IPs (skip-none).

destination_ip.description: Destination IP as a host string.

measured_at.description: When the measurement was produced (null when pending/dispatched).

measurement_id.description: Populated when the pair has a joined `measurements` row.

mtr_hops.description:
Inline MTR hops — populated iff `mtr_id` resolves to an
`mtr_traces` row.

pair_kind.description:
Kind of work the pair represents
(`campaign`, `detail_ping`, `detail_mtr`).

oneOf.description: Protocol of the joined measurement (null when pending/dispatched).

source_agent_id.description: Source agent id from the pair envelope.

CampaignMeasurementsPage.description: One page of the Raw-tab's joined campaign+measurements feed.

entries.description: Entries in `(measured_at DESC NULLS LAST, pair_id DESC)` order.

next_cursor.description:
Opaque cursor for the next page, or `null` when this is the
final page. Only populated when the final row has a non-null
`measured_at` (pending rows cannot be resumed via keyset).

CampaignState.description:
Lifecycle state of a measurement campaign. Mirrors the
`campaign_state` Postgres enum.

oneOf.description:
A campaign transitioned to a new lifecycle state. Emitted by the
[`super::listener`] task after parsing a `campaign_state_changed`
NOTIFY and resolving the current `state` column.

campaign_id.description: Primary key of the campaign whose state changed.

oneOf.description:
A pair belonging to this campaign reached a terminal resolution.
Emitted by the [`super::listener`] task on every
`campaign_pair_settled` NOTIFY. The payload intentionally omits
the pair id — subscribers re-fetch the affected pairs through
the existing `/api/campaigns/{id}/pairs` endpoint rather than
attempting to reconstruct state from the SSE stream alone.

campaign_id.description: Primary key of the campaign owning the settled pair.

oneOf.description:
The campaign's `campaign_evaluations` row was rewritten. Emitted
by the `POST /api/campaigns/:id/evaluate` handler after the
evaluation is persisted. Distinct from `StateChanged` because
re-evaluating an already-`Evaluated` campaign does not transition
state, yet must still invalidate the frontend's evaluation query
cache.

campaign_id.description: Primary key of the campaign whose evaluation was rewritten.

CampaignStreamEvent.description:
Campaign lifecycle event delivered to every SSE subscriber.

The `tag = "kind"` serde representation matches the wire shape the
frontend expects: one top-level `kind` discriminant plus flat
per-variant fields. Keep variant names in `snake_case` on the wire.

CatalogueCoordinates.description:
Latitude / longitude pair sourced from the IP catalogue join.

Present only when both coordinates are known; otherwise the parent
`AgentSummary.catalogue_coordinates` is `None`.

CatalogueEntryDto.description:
Operator-facing view of a single catalogue row.

This is the wire shape returned by `GET /api/catalogue/{id}` and
embedded in [`ListResponse::entries`] / [`PasteResponse::created`].

created_by.description: Operator principal (session username) that created the row.

display_name.description: Operator-supplied display label. Absent when unset.

enriched_at.description: Timestamp of the most recent successful enrichment run.

hostname.description:
Server-joined reverse-DNS hostname for [`Self::ip`].

Populated by the handler after a single batched
[`crate::hostname::hostnames_for`] lookup: `Some(_)` is a positive
cache hit; `None` is either a confirmed-negative hit (serialized
as absent via the skip-none attribute) or a cold miss. Cold
misses also enqueue a background resolution scoped to the
caller's session so the value arrives over the hostname SSE
stream.

ip.description:
Catalogued IP, rendered via `IpAddr::to_string()` (no CIDR
prefix — the row is always a host address).

operator_edited_fields.description:
Columns the operator has explicitly edited. PascalCase strings
matching [`super::model::Field::as_str`].

oneOf.description: A new catalogue row was inserted.

id.description: Primary key of the newly-inserted row.

ip.description:
Textual rendering of the catalogued IP for convenient client-side
display without a second fetch.

oneOf.description: An existing row's columns changed (operator edit or enrichment write).

id.description: Primary key of the updated row.

oneOf.description: A row was deleted.

id.description: Primary key of the row that was removed.

oneOf.description: Enrichment pipeline advanced for a row.

id.description: Primary key of the row whose enrichment status changed.

CatalogueEvent.description:
Catalogue lifecycle event delivered to every SSE subscriber.

The `tag = "kind"` serde representation matches the wire shape the
frontend expects: one top-level `kind` discriminant plus flat
per-variant fields. Keep variant names in `snake_case` on the wire —
`utoipa` and serde both honour `rename_all = "snake_case"`.

count.description: Number of rows with this city.
Number of rows with this country_code.

oneOf.description: Optional evaluation strategy.

force_measurement.description: When `true`, the scheduler ignores the 24 h reuse cache.

loss_threshold_ratio.description: Optional loss-rate threshold for the evaluator (fraction 0.0–1.0).

max_transit_rtt_ms.description: Optional eligibility cap on composed transit RTT (ms).

max_transit_stddev_ms.description: Optional eligibility cap on composed transit RTT stddev (ms).

min_improvement_ms.description: Optional storage floor on absolute improvement (ms).

min_improvement_ratio.description: Optional storage floor on relative improvement (fraction 0.0–1.0).

protocol.description: Probe protocol shared by every pair.

source_agent_ids.description: Source agent ids that will probe.

stddev_weight.description: Optional RTT-stddev weight for the evaluator.

destination_ip.description: Destination IP of the pair to re-measure.

source_agent_id.description: Source agent id of the pair to re-measure.

oneOf.description: Required iff scope == "pair". Rejected on other scopes.

scope.description: Which slice of candidates to enqueue for re-measurement.

pairs_enqueued.description: Number of detail pairs enqueued by this request.

DetailScope.description: Which slice of candidates the detail-trigger handler should re-measure.

DirectSource.description:
Where an evaluation pair-detail's "direct A→B" baseline came from.

Mirrors the `pair_detail_direct_source` Postgres enum. The `/evaluate`
handler layers VM continuous-mesh baselines on top of the active-probe
`measurements` join for agent→agent pairs the campaign didn't cover
itself, stamping [`Self::VmContinuous`] on the synthesized rows. When
both sources exist for the same pair, active-probe wins.

add_pairs.description: Pairs to add (or reset to `pending` if they already exist).

force_measurement.description:
When `Some(true)`, flips the sticky `force_measurement` flag and
resets every non-delta pair so the whole campaign re-runs.

EditPairDto.description:
A single `(source_agent, destination_ip)` pair identifier used by
the edit and force endpoints. `destination_ip` is a bare IP string.

destination_ip.description: Destination IP as a bare host string.

EnrichmentStatus.description: Current status of the enrichment pipeline for a row.

ErrorEnvelope.description: JSON error body returned by every non-2xx `/api/campaigns/*` response.

error.description: Snake-case error code; stable across versions.

avg_improvement_ms.description:
Average improvement (ms) across considered pairs. Defined as
`direct_rtt − transit_rtt − (transit_stddev_penalty − direct_stddev_penalty)`,
so a positive value means the transit candidate is faster than
the direct A→B baseline.

avg_loss_ratio.description:
Average compound loss (fraction 0.0–1.0) across transit triples
that cleared the loss gate during scoring.

composite_score.description:
Composite score `(pairs_improved / baseline_pair_count) ×
avg_improvement_ms`; higher is better. Candidates are returned
in descending composite-score order.

country_code.description: Catalogue ISO country code, when present.

destination_ip.description: Transit destination IP as a bare host string.

display_name.description: Operator-facing label from the catalogue, when present.

hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname for the transit destination IP, when cached.
Absent on cold miss and negative-cached IPs (skip-none).

is_mesh_member.description:
True when destination_ip appears in agents.ip. UI renders a
"mesh member — no acquisition needed" badge.

pair_details.description: Per-pair scoring detail for this candidate.

pairs_improved.description: Number of baseline pairs this candidate improved.

pairs_total_considered.description: Number of baseline pairs this candidate was scored against.

EvaluationDto.description:
Wire shape for `GET /api/campaigns/{id}/evaluation`.

Assembled from the relational `campaign_evaluations` parent row
plus its child tables (`campaign_evaluation_candidates`,
`campaign_evaluation_pair_details`,
`campaign_evaluation_unqualified_reasons`) by
[`crate::campaign::evaluation_repo::latest_evaluation_for_campaign`].
The read-path joins in Rust so the wire DTO stays unchanged
compared to the pre-T54-02 JSONB layout, modulo the new
`direct_source` field on every `pair_detail`.

avg_improvement_ms.description:
Average end-to-end improvement (ms) across qualifying candidates.
Same sign convention as
[`EvaluationCandidateDto::avg_improvement_ms`] — positive means
the transit beats the direct A→B baseline. `None` when no
candidate qualified.

baseline_pair_count.description: Number of `(source, destination)` baseline pairs considered.

candidates_good.description: Candidate transit destinations that cleared the `qualifies` bar.

evaluated_at.description: When the evaluator produced this result set.

evaluation_mode.description: Evaluation strategy that produced this result.

max_transit_rtt_ms.description:
Snapshot of [`CampaignDto::max_transit_rtt_ms`] at `/evaluate`
time. `None` means the eligibility cap was disabled for this
evaluation pass. Persisted on `campaign_evaluations` so each
historical evaluation row carries the guardrails that produced
it, even after later PATCHes change the campaign-level value.

stddev_weight.description: Weight applied to RTT stddev during scoring.

EvaluationMode.description:
Evaluation strategy for the campaign's result-aggregation pass
(spec 04). Storage here; consumed by T48.

destination_agent_id.description: Destination agent id of the baseline pair.

destination_hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname for the transit destination IP, when cached.
Absent on cold miss and negative-cached IPs (skip-none).

destination_ip.description: Transit destination IP (also a candidate key).

direct_source.description:
Provenance of the direct A→B baseline figures:
[`DirectSource::ActiveProbe`] for rows that came from the
campaign's own `measurements`, or [`DirectSource::VmContinuous`]
when the evaluator pulled the A→B baseline from VictoriaMetrics
continuous-mesh data at `/evaluate` time. Transit legs (A→X,
X→B) are always active-probe; only the direct A→B baseline can
be VM-sourced.

improvement_ms.description:
`direct_rtt − transit_rtt − (transit_stddev_penalty −
direct_stddev_penalty)`; positive means the transit beats the
direct A→B baseline by that many ms after stddev-penalty
adjustment.

mtr_measurement_id_ax.description: FK to the `measurements` row covering A→X, when available.

mtr_measurement_id_xb.description: FK to the `measurements` row covering X→B, when available.

qualifies.description: Whether this pair cleared the evaluator's qualify predicate.

source_agent_id.description: Source agent id of the baseline pair.

EvaluationPairDetailListResponse.description:
Wire response body for
`GET /api/campaigns/{id}/evaluation/candidates/{destination_ip}/pair_details`.

`total` reflects the runtime filter set but ignores the cursor — it
is the size of the full filtered result set across pages, not the
remaining-after-cursor count, so a UI status bar can render
"showing N of TOTAL" with one number that doesn't drift mid-scroll.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or `None` at end-of-result.

total.description:
Total rows across the full filtered result set, ignoring the
cursor. Renderable as the "of TOTAL" half of a status bar.

candidates.description: Per-candidate scoring rows, ordered by composite score.

unqualified_reasons.description:
Keyed by destination IP (string); value is an explanatory
sentence the UI renders verbatim.

FacetsResponse.description: Aggregate facets used by the catalogue's filter UI.

asns.description: Top 250 ASN buckets, descending by count.

cities.description: Top 250 city buckets, descending by count.

countries.description: Top 250 country buckets, descending by count.

networks.description: Top 250 operator buckets, descending by count.

destination_ip.description: Destination IP of the pair to force.

source_agent_id.description: Source agent id of the pair to force.

HistoryDestinationDto.description:
One destination reachable from a given source.

`display_name` falls back to `host(destination_ip)` when no catalogue
row exists (either never enriched or later deleted). The frontend
renders this as "raw IP — no metadata", a supported state rather than
a rendering bug — `city`, `country_code`, and `asn` all stay NULL.

destination_ip.description: Raw destination IP as a host string.

display_name.description: Catalogue-derived label when known, else the IP string.

hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname for the destination IP, when cached.
Absent on cold miss and negative-cached IPs (skip-none).

is_mesh_member.description: Whether the destination IP is itself a mesh-agent IP.

HistoryMeasurementDto.description:
One joined `measurements` + optional `mtr_traces` row for the
`/history/pair` page.

`mtr_hops` decodes the `mtr_traces.hops` JSONB column into the typed
`Vec<HopJson>` wire shape. sqlx requires the `Json<_>` wrapper for
JSONB columns in `query_as!`; the wire JSON stays flat thanks to
`sqlx::types::Json`'s transparent serde impl.

destination_hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname for the destination IP, when cached.
Absent on cold miss and negative-cached IPs (skip-none).

destination_ip.description: Destination IP as a host string.

mtr_captured_at.description: When the associated `mtr_traces` row was captured.

mtr_hops.description: MTR hop array; populated when the measurement has an `mtr_id`.

probe_count.description: Number of probes in the sample.

source_agent_id.description: Source agent id that produced the row.

display_name.description:
Display name from `agents_with_catalogue` — the catalogue-derived
name when set, else the agent's own `display_name`, else the id.

HopIpJson.description: JSON representation of an observed IP at a hop.

freq.description: Fraction of probes that observed this IP at this hop.

hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname for this IP, populated server-side at
response-serialize time only; never written to the
`route_snapshots.hops` / `mtr_traces.hops` JSONB (guarded by
`skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none"`). Existing stored rows
deserialize with `hostname: None` without a migration.

ip.description: Human-readable IP string (IPv4 or IPv6 `to_string()` form).

HopJson.description:
JSON representation of a single traceroute hop as stored in
`route_snapshots.hops`.

avg_rtt_micros.description: Mean RTT to this hop, in microseconds.

loss_ratio.description: Fraction of probes with no response at this hop.

observed_ips.description: IP addresses observed at this hop and their frequencies.

position.description: 1-indexed TTL / hop position (matches the protocol contract).

stddev_rtt_micros.description: Standard deviation of RTT to this hop, in microseconds.

HostnameEvent.description:
Wire payload for the `hostname_resolved` SSE event.

`IpAddr` has no built-in `ToSchema` impl under utoipa 5, so we annotate
the field with `schema(value_type = String)`. Serde still uses the
default `IpAddr` display serializer, which matches the shape the
frontend expects (e.g. `"192.0.2.1"` / `"2001:db8::1"`).

hostname.description:
Resolved hostname, or `None` when the lookup confirmed no PTR
record exists.

ip.description: Canonicalized IP the lookup applies to.

query.description: PromQL expression. Must reference at least one `meshmon_` metric.

time.description: Optional evaluation timestamp (RFC 3339 or Unix epoch).

LatestByProtocol.description:
Per-protocol latest-snapshot map. Each field is independent so the UI can
render "icmp present, udp/tcp empty" without a sentinel value.

oneOf.description: Latest ICMP snapshot in window, if any.
Latest TCP snapshot in window, if any.
Latest UDP snapshot in window, if any.

entries.description:
Matching rows ordered by the request's `sort` / `sort_dir` with
`id DESC` as the tiebreaker; nullable sort columns place NULLs
at the tail regardless of direction.

next_cursor.description:
Forward-paging token. `Some` when the server filled `limit`
rows and a subsequent page may exist; `None` when the end of
the result set has been reached. See [`super::sort::Cursor`]
for the wire format.

total.description:
Count of all rows matching the filter (ignores `limit`).

When the `shapes` filter is non-empty, `total` is an upper-
bound approximation: SQL can only pre-filter by the shapes'
union bounding box, so rows inside the bbox but outside every
polygon are counted here while the corresponding point-in-
polygon pass drops them from `entries`. Clients that need an
exact post-shape count must sum `entries.len()` across every
page.

password.description:
Plaintext password. Verified against the PHC hash via argon2 inside
`spawn_blocking`.

MapBucket.description: One grid-aggregated cluster cell surfaced by [`MapResponse::Clusters`].

bbox.description: Cell bounding box as `[min_lat, min_lng, max_lat, max_lng]`.

sample_id.description:
A deterministically-chosen row id from inside the cell — useful
for client-side drill-down without re-querying the whole cell.

oneOf.description:
Raw rows — returned when the filtered count stays at or below
the detail threshold.

rows.description: Matching rows, ordered by `(created_at DESC, id DESC)`.

total.description: Count of all rows matching the filter within the viewport.

oneOf.description:
Grid-aggregated buckets — returned when the filtered count
exceeds the detail threshold.

buckets.description: One bucket per occupied grid cell.

cell_size.description:
Grid cell size in degrees; matches
[`super::repo::cell_size_for_zoom`] for the request's `zoom`.

total.description: Total row count in the viewport before aggregation.

MapResponse.description:
Adaptive response for `GET /api/catalogue/map`.

- `detail` — raw rows — when the filtered count in the request bbox
  is at or below [`super::repo::MAP_DETAIL_THRESHOLD`].
- `clusters` — grid-aggregated buckets — when above the threshold.

The wire form carries a `kind` discriminator (`"detail"` /
`"clusters"`) so clients can branch without inspecting the variant's
fields. See [`super::repo::map_detail_or_clusters`] for the
server-side selection logic.

MeasurementKind.description:
Kind of measurement row stored in `measurements`. `campaign` is the
default; T44 never writes anything else (T45/T48 do).

count.description: Number of rows with this operator.

PairDetailSortCol.description:
Sortable columns for the paginated pair_details endpoint
(`GET /api/campaigns/{id}/evaluation/candidates/{destination_ip}/pair_details`).

The set is closed: every variant maps to a hardcoded SQL fragment in
[`crate::campaign::evaluation_repo::latest_pair_details_for_candidate`]
so user input never reaches the SQL string. Adding a sort column
requires extending the enum, the SQL builder's `match`, and the
composite-index migration if a new index is justified.

PairDetailSortDir.description:
Sort direction for [`PairDetailSortCol`]. The composite-PK tiebreak
`(source_agent_id, destination_agent_id)` is always ascending; only
the leading column flips.

PairDto.description: Wire shape for a single pair in `GET /api/campaigns/{id}/pairs`.

attempt_count.description: Number of dispatch attempts to date.

destination_hostname.description:
Reverse-DNS hostname for the destination IP, when cached.
Absent on cold miss and negative-cached IPs (skip-none).

destination_ip.description: Destination IP as a bare host string.

dispatched_at.description: When the scheduler dispatched the pair to an agent.

last_error.description: Last error observed on this pair, if any.

measurement_id.description: FK to the `measurements` row once dispatched or reused.

settled_at.description: When the pair reached a terminal state.

PairResolutionState.description:
Terminal or in-progress state of a single `(source_agent_id,
destination_ip)` pair within a campaign.

reason.description: Short human-readable reason — intended for immediate UI display.

token.description: The exact token as received from the client.

PasteMetadata.description:
Operator-set default metadata applied to every accepted IP in a
paste.

Each field is independently optional — absent means "don't touch
this column". Two pairings are **atomic**: `latitude`+`longitude`
must be supplied together (or both omitted), and
`country_code`+`country_name` must be supplied together. The
paste handler rejects half-supplied pairs with 400
`paired_metadata_half_missing`.

country_code.description:
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code. Must be paired with
[`PasteMetadata::country_name`]. Validated as a 2-character
ASCII-alphabetic string by the handler.

country_name.description:
Country human-readable name. Must be paired with
[`PasteMetadata::country_code`].

latitude.description:
Decimal latitude in [-90, 90]. Must be paired with
[`PasteMetadata::longitude`].

longitude.description:
Decimal longitude in [-180, 180]. Must be paired with
[`PasteMetadata::latitude`].

PasteRequest.description:
Paste payload — a raw list of IP tokens. Each token is parsed by
[`super::parse::parse_ip_tokens`]; tokens may be bare IPs or host
CIDRs (`/32` for v4, `/128` for v6). Wider CIDRs and unparseable
tokens fall into [`PasteResponse::invalid`] instead of aborting the
whole request.

An optional [`PasteMetadata`] applies the same operator-set fields
to every accepted IP. Newly-created rows always receive the values
and have the corresponding field names appended to
`operator_edited_fields`. Existing rows receive a field only if it
is not already locked; paired fields (`Latitude`+`Longitude` and
`CountryCode`+`CountryName`) apply atomically — if either half of
a pair is locked, neither half is written. Requests without
`metadata` preserve the pre-T52 behaviour exactly (no writes to
`existing` rows, no `skipped_summary` on the response).

ips.description: Raw tokens to parse and (when valid) insert into the catalogue.

oneOf.description:
Optional default metadata applied to every accepted IP. See the
struct-level docs for the merge semantics.

per_ip_display_names.description:
Optional per-IP display-name overrides, keyed by the caller's
literal IP string (same form as it appears in `ips`). An
override supplies the display name for a single IP and wins
over `metadata.display_name`; it still honours the lock rule on
existing rows (an existing `DisplayName` lock blocks the
override just like it blocks the panel default). Keys that do
not parse as IPs, or that are not in the accepted set, are
silently ignored — matches the permissive posture used by the
keyset cursor and `ip_prefix` filter.

PasteResponse.description:
Response body for `POST /api/catalogue` — a three-way split of the
paste outcome plus an optional aggregate describing metadata
writes that were skipped against existing rows.

created.description: Rows newly inserted by this call.

existing.description:
Rows already present in the catalogue. When the request
carried a [`PasteMetadata`], each entry here reflects the
post-merge row state — so the UI does not need a follow-up
fetch to observe writes that survived the lock check.

oneOf.description:
Aggregate summary of metadata writes that were refused because
the target field was already operator-locked. Absent when the
request did not carry `metadata`; present (and possibly
all-zero) otherwise.

PasteSkippedSummary.description:
Aggregate describing metadata writes the server refused to apply
because the target row already carried operator locks on the
affected fields.

Keys in [`PasteSkippedSummary::skipped_field_counts`] are canonical
[`super::model::Field::as_str`] names, plus two composite labels
for paired fields:
- `"Location"` — either half of `Latitude`/`Longitude` was locked.
- `"Country"`  — either half of `CountryCode`/`CountryName` was
  locked.

The composite names let the UI narrate skips without reconstructing
the pairing client-side.

rows_with_skips.description: Count of rows that kept at least one operator-locked field.

skipped_field_counts.description: Per-field skip counts; zero-count keys are omitted.

PatchCampaignRequest.description:
PATCH body for `/api/campaigns/{id}`.

Absent fields leave the existing column untouched. There is no
revert-to-auto surface here because campaigns only persist
operator-authored data.

oneOf.description: Replacement evaluation strategy.

max_transit_rtt_ms.description: Replacement eligibility cap on composed transit RTT (ms).

max_transit_stddev_ms.description: Replacement eligibility cap on composed transit RTT stddev (ms).

min_improvement_ms.description: Replacement storage floor on absolute improvement (ms).

min_improvement_ratio.description: Replacement storage floor on relative improvement (fraction 0.0–1.0).

PatchRequest.description:
PATCH payload for `PATCH /api/catalogue/{id}` (declared here for T12
so all catalogue wire shapes live in one module).

Triple-state field encoding (outer `Option` = touched?, inner
`Option` = NULL?) mirrors [`super::repo::PatchValue`]. Callers omit
the JSON key for "leave untouched", send `null` for "set NULL",
and send a concrete value for "set to this".

display_name.description: New display name. See the struct doc for triple-state encoding.

revert_to_auto.description:
Names of fields the operator wants reverted to automatic
enrichment. Values must match [`super::model::Field::as_str`].

PathMetrics.description: VictoriaMetrics-sourced RTT / loss series for the primary protocol.

loss_current.description: Last loss value (fraction), or null if the series is empty.

rtt_current.description: Last RTT value (ms), or null if the series is empty.

PathOverviewResponse.description: Aggregated response for the Path Detail page.

latest_by_protocol.description: Latest snapshot per protocol within the window (each field optional).

oneOf.description:
VM series for the primary protocol, or `null` when the VM was
unreachable or misconfigured.

primary_protocol.description:
Server-picked primary protocol (`icmp`, `udp`, or `tcp`). `null`
only when no protocol has any snapshot in the window; callers should
treat that as "show a neutral empty state".

recent_snapshots.description:
Recent snapshots in the window in descending `observed_at` order,
filtered to the resolved [`PathOverviewResponse::primary_protocol`].
Capped at [`RECENT_LIMIT`]; no hop detail. Empty when no primary
protocol could be resolved (no data for any protocol in the
window).

recent_snapshots_truncated.description:
`true` when the per-primary-protocol `recent_snapshots` list was
clamped at [`RECENT_LIMIT`]. Since the list is protocol-scoped
from the start, this flag is always honest for the operator-visible
banner ("narrow the window for more").

PathSummaryJson.description:
JSON representation of the aggregated path summary stored in
`route_snapshots.path_summary`.

avg_rtt_micros.description: Mean RTT across all hops, in microseconds.

hop_count.description: Total number of hops in the route.

Polygon.description:
GeoJSON-compatible polygon ring expressed as `[lng, lat]` pairs.

The ring is implicitly closed — the `TryFrom` impl below does not
require an explicit closing vertex. A minimum of three *distinct*
points is required; shorter rings (two vertices or all-identical
points) are rejected as [`ShapeError::TooFewPoints`].

The `[lng, lat]` order matches GeoJSON and `@turf/helpers`, so the
frontend can feed Turf outputs straight through without reordering.

fresh.description: Pairs the scheduler would dispatch fresh.

reusable.description: Pairs resolvable from the 24 h reuse window.

total.description: Total number of `(source, destination)` pairs that would be created.

ProbeProtocol.description: The probe protocol an individual pair uses.

query.description: PromQL expression. Must reference at least one `meshmon_` metric.

RouteSnapshotDetail.description: Full detail of a single route snapshot (includes hops).

oneOf.description: Aggregated path summary (optional).

protocol.description: Protocol used for the traceroute (icmp, tcp, udp).

RouteSnapshotSummary.description: Summary of a route snapshot (no hops — used in list responses).

oneOf.description: Aggregated path summary (optional).

protocol.description: Protocol used for the traceroute (icmp, tcp, udp).

RoutesPage.description: Paginated response of route snapshot summaries.

SessionResponse.description:
Minimal session probe response.

Write-only on the server — we only ever construct and serialize this
type, never parse one back in — so only `Serialize` is derived.

agents.description:
Thresholds the SPA needs to interpret `AgentSummary.last_seen_at`
without baking the values into the bundle. See
[`AgentLivenessConfig`] for the contract.

username.description:
Signed-in username. The SPA hydrates its auth store from this
so a hard-refresh tab still knows who's signed in.

version.description:
`CARGO_PKG_VERSION` of the running service — surfaced so the SPA
can warn when the user's loaded bundle is older than the server.

SortBy.description:
Sort columns accepted by `GET /api/catalogue`.

The list handler always appends `id DESC` as the tiebreaker so the
ordering is total even when multiple rows share the same sort
value, and every variant treats nullable columns as `NULLS LAST`
regardless of direction. Both invariants are load-bearing for the
keyset cursor — see [`super::sort::Cursor`].

SortDir.description: Sort direction for [`SortBy`]. `NULLS LAST` applies to both arms.

WindowBounds.description: Inclusive time window bounds echoed back to the caller.
